basic_info.title: Bravely Default II
basic_info.review: Bravely Default II is a good RPG that undermines everything amazing the previous two games with its new battle style discouraging risk-taking and less freedom, though it features stunning visuals and an innovative soundtrack.

alternative_names: Bravely Default II (ブレイブリーデフォルトII)

play_today.details: The definitive version with superior performance, up to 120FPS support, 4K resolution options, and enhanced graphical settings. PCGamingWiki confirms full Steam Deck compatibility with cloud save support.

recommended_patches.name: Ultrawide Fix Mod
recommended_patches.description: DLL hook that adds ultrawide/custom resolution support with centered 16:9 HUD. Available via GitHub and GameBrew.

recommended_patches.name: 60 FPS Mod
recommended_patches.description: Community mod that enables higher frame rates beyond the default 30FPS cap for smoother gameplay on capable hardware.

recommended_patches.name: ReShade Preset
recommended_patches.description: Enhances visual quality with improved lighting, colors, and sharpness for a more polished appearance.

play_today.details: Original release with all content but limited to 30FPS and lower resolution (1080p docked, 720p portable). Performance issues include frame drops during quest acceptance and dialogue scenes.

recommended_patches.name: 60 FPS Mod Switch
recommended_patches.description: Community mod that changes target FPS to 60. Higher GPU overclock recommended for better resolution.

recommended_patches.name: System Update
recommended_patches.description: Official Nintendo system update to ensure optimal performance and bug fixes.

optimal_settings: Use accurate CPU timing for best compatibility
High performance mode for smoother gameplay

play_today.details: Runs via backward compatibility with improved frame stability compared to original Switch, though no native version exists yet. Performance issues reported by some users including skipping and frame loss.

recommended_patches.name: 60 FPS Mod Switch
recommended_patches.description: Community mod that changes target FPS to 60. Higher GPU overclock recommended for better resolution.

description.synopsis: Four heroes—Seth the sailor, Gloria the princess, Elvis the scholar, and Adelle the mercenary—journey across the continent of Excillant to recover four elemental crystals stolen by the Kingdom of Holograd while confronting an ancient evil known as the Night's Nexus.
key_features: Updated Brave/Default battle system with improved job customization
Unreal Engine 4 graphics with stunning visual presentation
Separate story and cast from previous Bravely games (standalone entry)
Multiple endings based on player choices and completion requirements
Asterisk-based job system with enhanced character development options
Mysterious book that serves as both save system and narrative device
description.long_description: Bravely Default II is a 2021 role-playing video game developed by Claytechworks and published by Square Enix. It was released worldwide for the Nintendo Switch on February 26, 2021, with Nintendo publishing outside of Japan, and for Windows via Steam on September 3, 2021. While it is the third main installment in the Bravely series following the original Bravely Default (2012) and its direct sequel Bravely Second: End Layer (2015), it features a completely new story, setting, and cast of characters set on the continent of Excillant. The game was announced at The Game Awards in December 2019, with series producer Tomoya Asano explaining that despite being technically the third entry, it was titled Bravely Default II because he felt Bravely Second did not meet fan expectations. Revo, composer for the original Bravely Default (but not Bravely Second), returned to compose the soundtrack. The PC version supports framerates up to 120FPS compared to 30FPS on Switch, resolutions up to 4K, faster loading times, and more detailed graphical settings. It was later verified to have full Steam Deck compatibility including cloud save support. As of December 2021, Square Enix confirmed the game had sold over one million units across both platforms.
legacy_and_impact: Returned Revo as composer after Bravely Second's different musical direction
Demonstrated commercial viability for standalone entries in established JRPG series
Introduced Unreal Engine 4 to the Bravely franchise with significant visual upgrade
Maintained strong community engagement despite mixed reception compared to original
Led to continued development interest in the Bravely series by Square Enix
